name: Huracan
shortDescription: Huracan is the Mayan god of wind, storm, and fire, and one of the creator deities who participated in the various attempts to create humankind.
description: Huracan, also spelled Huracán, is a powerful and complex figure in Mayan mythology. He is primarily associated with wind, storms, and fire, embodying destructive and creative forces. In the Popol Vuh, the Mayan creation myth, Huracan is one of the three creator gods who bring about the various attempts to create humanity. He is also described as the 'Heart of Sky' (or 'Heart of Heaven'), suggesting his importance and central role in the cosmos. Huracan is often depicted as a whirlwind or a powerful storm, representing the chaotic and unpredictable nature of the natural world. He played a crucial role in the destruction of the wooden people, the second creation attempt, by unleashing a great flood. This act underscores his power and his role as an agent of divine judgment. While destructive, Huracan's actions are ultimately part of the larger creation cycle, paving the way for the successful creation of humans from maize. His connection to wind and storms links him to agricultural cycles, as wind brings rain and influences crop growth. The name 'hurricane' in English is derived from Huracan, a testament to the lasting impact of Mayan culture and mythology on modern languages and our understanding of powerful storms. His association with fire further connects him to destruction and purification, and his role in creation highlights the cyclical nature of Mayan cosmology, where destruction is often a necessary precursor to new beginnings. Huracan is a central figure in understanding Mayan beliefs about creation, destruction, and the natural world.

sources.title: Maya Cosmos: Three Thousand Years on the Shaman's Path
sources.author: David Freidel, Linda Schele, and Joy Parker

sources.title: A Forest of Kings: The Untold Story of the Ancient Maya
sources.author: Linda Schele and David Freidel
